sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Stephanie Morales, a 36-year-old female, presents with a complaint of ankle pain.
She reports that she slipped and twisted her left ankle two days ago while walking on ice.
She fell and was able to get up with assistance, but experienced significant soreness, swelling, and pain, making it hard to walk.
She has tried ibuprofen, ice, and elevation, but has only experienced a little relief.
The pain is localized to the outside of her ankle, with no pain or tenderness in her calf, lower leg, or knee.
Past medical history: She has a history of diabetes and high blood pressure.
Review of systems: No other symptoms are mentioned.
Current medications: She is taking metformin and norvasc.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 120/70.
Physical exam: The patient's neck, lungs, and heart exams are normal.
She has a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, which has been unchanged from previous exams.
Her belly exam is normal.
On her ankle exam, she has tenderness over the lateral malleolus and the fifth metatarsal.
There is some swelling, redness, and pain with valgus stressing of her ankle.
She has normal sensation and pulses in her ankle.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I diagnose the patient with an ankle sprain, but I am concerned about a possible fracture due to tenderness over the fifth metatarsal.
I will order an x-ray to confirm the diagnosis.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; possible fracture of the fifth metatarsal; diabetes; high blood pressure.
Plan:
I will order an x-ray of the ankle to confirm the diagnosis.
I will put the patient in an air splint and provide her with crutches until the x-ray results are available.
I will prescribe naprosyn 500mg twice a day for pain control.
I will instruct her to keep her ankle elevated, apply ice, and avoid weightbearing.
If the x-ray shows a fracture, I will refer her to orthopedics.
Follow-up: I will contact the patient once the x-ray results are available to discuss the next steps.
I will also instruct her to follow up with her existing appointment with the ophthalmologist for her diabetes checkup.
I will refill her norvasc and order another hemoglobin A1c to check her blood sugar levels.
